signals.title: European Commission's Guidelines on the Protection of Minors under the Digital Services Act (DSA)
signals.summary: These guidelines advise online platforms to implement measures to protect minors, such as setting accounts to private by default and enhancing content moderation. They aim to safeguard minors' personal information and reduce exposure to harmful content.
key_provisions: Set minors' accounts to private by default
Modify recommender systems to reduce harmful content exposure
Enhance content moderation and reporting tools

signals.title: Development of an EU Age Verification Solution
signals.summary: The European Commission released a blueprint for a privacy-preserving age verification solution, allowing users to prove they are over 18 without disclosing additional personal information.
key_provisions: Supports DSA provisions for minor protection

signals.title: European Commission's Scrutiny of Platforms' Safeguards for Minors
signals.summary: The European Commission initiated investigative actions to assess the age verification systems of platforms like Snapchat and YouTube, ensuring minors are protected from illegal or harmful content.
key_provisions: Investigative actions on age verification systems
Focus on preventing minors from accessing harmful content

signals.title: Italy's Draft Law on the Protection of Minors in the Digital Dimension
signals.summary: This draft law proposes restricting access to social media and video-sharing platforms to individuals over 15 and mandates age verification mechanisms.
key_provisions: Restrict access to social media for under 15

signals.title: Spain's Draft Organic Law for the Protection of Minors in Digital Environments
signals.summary: This draft law requires device manufacturers to include parental controls by default and prohibits random reward mechanisms for minors.
key_provisions: Prohibit random reward mechanisms for minors
